Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Phase 2: Implement LangGraph Native Interrupts for Human-in-the-Loop

Replace file-based polling system with LangGraph''s native interrupt\(\) function
for cleaner, more reliable human interaction with automatic state persistence.

Tasks Completed \(2.0.0.3 - 2.0.0.11\):
- Task 2.0.0.3: Rewrote _request_human_review_node with interrupt\(\)
- Task 2.0.0.4: Added _ask_human_question\(\) helper method
- Task 2.0.0.5: Implemented validation loop with _interrupt_with_validation\(\)
- Task 2.0.0.6: Updated graph routing for interrupt handling
- Task 2.0.0.7: Removed wait_for_human and process_human_response nodes
- Task 2.0.0.8: Created CLI interrupt handler module
- Task 2.0.0.9: Updated main run loop for interrupt support
- Task 2.0.0.10: Deprecated file-based HumanLoopManager
- Task 2.0.0.11: Wrote comprehensive interrupt tests \(24 tests\)

Key Changes:

orchestrator.py:
- Added validation functions: _validate_review_response\(\), _validate_question_response\(\)
- Added _interrupt_with_validation\(\) for retry loop on invalid input
- Added _ask_human_question\(\) helper using interrupt\(\)
- Rewrote _request_human_review_node\(\) to use interrupt\(\) instead of polling
- Updated graph routing to go directly from request_human_review to action nodes
- Removed _wait_for_human_node and _process_human_response_node \(80+ lines deleted\)
- Pre-interrupt code is idempotent \(git status/diff reads only\)

interrupt_handler.py \(NEW\):
- display_interrupt\(\) - Routes to appropriate display function
- _display_review_request\(\) - Rich formatted review output
- _display_question\(\) - Rich formatted question output
- collect_response\(\) - Routes to appropriate collection function
- _collect_review_response\(\) - Interactive approve/retry/skip with feedback
- _collect_question_response\(\) - Interactive answer collection with options

cli.py:
- Added run_with_interrupts\(\) async function for interrupt-aware execution
- Implements detection loop checking for __interrupt__ in graph results
- Calls display_interrupt\(\) and collect_response\(\) on interrupts
- Resumes with Command\(resume=response\)
- Tracks tasks_processed and respects max_tasks limit
- Updated cmd_run\(\) to use new interrupt-aware function

human_loop.py:
- Added deprecation notice to module docstring
- Added DeprecationWarning in HumanLoopManager.__init__
- Kept for backwards compatibility with batch processing

test_orchestrator_interrupts.py \(NEW\):
- TestInterruptPayloads: 3 tests for payload structure
- TestInterruptValidation: 11 tests for response validation
- TestInterruptHandler: 9 tests for CLI handler functions
- TestAskHumanQuestion: 1 test for question helper
- All 24 tests passing

Benefits:
- Better integration with LangGraph state management
- Automatic state persistence across interrupts
- Cleaner code without polling loops
- Interactive CLI prompts instead of file watching
- Input validation with automatic re-prompting
- Idempotent pre-interrupt code safe for graph replays

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
imports: Fix cross-file namespace generation for multi-file projects

Compute source root path dynamically from actual source files instead
of hardcoding "src" subdirectory. This allows imports between files
in the same directory to resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
test: Add multi-file compilation integration tests

Add tests that demonstrate multi-file import scenarios:
- import_with_classes: Tests import/from-import in same file
- module_import_access: Tests module.function\(\) access pattern

These tests verify the cross-file namespace generation fix works
for realistic multi-file projects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
